mission.title: openfunction: [Discussion] Integration possibility of bundled go2sky
mission.description: [Discussion] Integration possibility of bundled go2sky. Requested by 2+ users.

steps.title: Check current openfunction deployment
steps.description:
Verify your openfunction version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n openfunction -l app.kubernetes.io/name=openfunction
helm list -n openfunction 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working openfunction installation.

steps.title: Review openfunction configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant openfunction configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n openfunction -l app.kubernetes.io/name=openfunction
kubectl get configmap -n openfunction -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=openfunction
```
Hi , I want to set up this thread talking about the tech possibility of bundled go2sky.

FaaS is basically highly distributed, even more complex than micro-services or meshed services.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Discussion] Integration possibility of bundled go2sky
steps.description:
> Good to know this. If there are somethings need my help, please let me know. The general idea is providing logic service concept and function as SkyWalking endpoint in OpenFunction core. Then, in default, users wouldn't require manual codes to make tracing, topology, metrics out of box. Then, the next step could be logging and tracing collaborations, which OpenFunction core could inject the context into logs, and ship logs into SkyWalking for further process. This should be not hard 😄 As

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n openfunction -l app.kubernetes.io/name=openfunction
kubectl get events -n openfunction --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Discussion] Integration possibility of bundled go2sky" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: > Good to know this. If there are somethings need my help, please let me know. The general idea is providing logic service concept and function as SkyWalking endpoint in OpenFunction core. Then, in default, users wouldn't require manual codes to make tracing, topology, metrics out of box.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with openfunction installed or the issue environment reproducible.
